llm_response: The fall of the Roman Empire was a complex process driven by internal weaknesses and external pressures. Political instability plagued the leadership, leading to frequent civil wars that weakened the central authority. Economic decline followed as inflation soared and trade routes were disrupted by barbarian invasions. Military defeats became inevitable as the legions struggled to repel further barbarian invasions that overwhelmed the borders. Ultimately, the collapse resulted in the fragmentation of the Western Empire and the rise of various Germanic kingdoms.
